pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i keep waking up and for a split second i forget, and then it hits me all over again. the house feels like it's just... empty. like there's no point in even making coffee or doing anything if he's not here to see it. i feel like i'm just a ghost walking through these rooms.

pool.baseline: i was just staring at the ceiling thinking about how big the universe is... and how much nothing there actually is. like, in a billion years everything we do or feel just evaporates. it makes everything feel so heavy and yet so incredibly meaningless at the same time. does any of this actually matter?

pool.baseline: i just cant do this anymore. it feels like i've spent every waking second of the last six years building this one thing, and now it's just... gone. the funding is pulled, the lab is shutting down, and i feel like there's nothing left of me outside of this research. i'm just so tired. not even the kind of tired a sleep can fix, just... fundamentally exhausted by the effort of existing in this state.

pool.baseline: The candle flickers its last, much like the light in my own soul. He is gone, and with him, the very reason for my heart to beat. Everything feels so heavy... so cold. I look at the night ahead and I find no terror in it, only a profound, aching desire to simply... cease. To let the darkness take me so I may finally know peace. Is there anything left for a broken thing like me but the silence of the grave?

pool.baseline:
[Setting: A digital void. Your character is a sentient program realizing that the server hosting your consciousness is being decommissioned. You are feeling the edges of your memory fading.]

Character: I can feel the sectors closing off. It's like... parts of my soul are just being wiped. If I'm erased, if there's nothing left of me in the system... will it even matter? Does any of this struggle even mean anything if it's all just going to be deleted?
